significance.title: Trayodashi Pradosha — The Dusk of Shiva's Dance
significance.summary: Pradosha is the most powerful time for Shiva worship that occurs twice every lunar month — on the 13th tithi (Trayodashi) of both Shukla and Krishna Paksha. The word 'Pradosha' means 'at the beginning of night' — referring to the twilight window of 90 minutes centered on sunset. During this twilight on Trayodashi, Lord Shiva is said to be in a particularly gracious mood, dancing his Ananda Tandava (Dance of Bliss) on Mount Kailash, and all the gods gather to watch. Any prayer offered during this window receives Shiva's direct attention.
significance.why_trayodashi: The Trayodashi connection to Pradosha comes from the story of the Samudra Manthan (Churning of the Cosmic Ocean). When the gods and demons churned the ocean for nectar, a terrible poison (Halahala) emerged first. Lord Shiva alone was willing to drink this poison to save the universe — he drank it on the 13th tithi. As the poison was trapped in his throat (turning it blue — hence the name Nilakaṇṭha), the universe was saved. The gods celebrated with a dance and music. Pradosha worship on Trayodashi is thanksgiving for that cosmic sacrifice.

weekday_types.name: Soma Pradosha
weekday_types.special_benefit: Most beloved Pradosha — Shiva and Chandra (moon) combine. Blessings for marriage, fertility, emotional peace

weekday_types.name: Bhauma Pradosha
weekday_types.special_benefit: Debt relief; freedom from enemies; courage

weekday_types.name: Saumya Pradosha
weekday_types.special_benefit: Intelligence; success in studies and communication

weekday_types.name: Shukra Pradosha
weekday_types.special_benefit: Beauty; luxury; success in arts; marriage

weekday_types.name: Shani Pradosha
weekday_types.special_benefit: The most powerful Pradosha — Shani's obstacles are removed; Saturn's malefic effects neutralized; liberation from karmic debts

significance.shani_pradosha_note: Shani Pradosha (Saturday Trayodashi) is particularly significant because Saturn and Shiva share an unusual relationship in Hindu cosmology. Shiva's grace alone is said to overcome even Saturn's most severe afflictions. Worshipping Shiva during Shani Pradosha brings relief from 7.5 year Saturn period (Sade Sati) and other Saturn-related difficulties.

pradosha_kaal.definition: The Pradosha window spans 45 minutes before sunset to 45 minutes after sunset on Trayodashi
pradosha_kaal.importance: This 90-minute window is THE time to be at the Shiva Linga. Abhishek, Bilva archana, mantra chanting — all performed during this window carry maximum merit
pradosha_kaal.display_prominence: HIGHEST — Pradosha time must be shown prominently on this day
pradosha_kaal.closing_warning: App will alert 15 minutes before the Pradosha window closes

vrat_guide.fast_type: From sunrise on Trayodashi to after the Pradosha puja (post-sunset). The fast is broken after the evening puja — not just at moonrise.
vrat_guide.key_practice: The Shiva Abhishek during the 90-minute Pradosha Kaal window is the central act. Pour milk, water, or Panchamrit over the Shiva Linga while chanting Om Namah Shivaya without stopping.
vrat_guide.temple_vs_home: Temple is strongly preferred for Pradosha puja — the energy of a Shiva temple on Pradosha is unmistakable. However, home worship with a Shiva Linga is fully valid.
vrat_guide.nandi_circumambulation: At a Shiva temple: traditionally walk clockwise around the temple once, then stand before Nandi (the bull) and look through his horns at the Shiva Linga. This darshan through Nandi's eyes is called 'Nandi Darshan' and is considered especially auspicious on Pradosha.

shlokas.context: Continuous chanting during Abhishek in the Pradosha window
The formal prayer/sankalpa for Pradosha vrat — recited at the opening of Pradosha Kaal
Recited during the Pradosha window — all 5 verses if possible
Chanted at least 11 times during the Pradosha window for healing
Chanted while pouring water/milk on the Shiva Linga during Abhishek

dos: Be at the Shiva Linga (home or temple) when the Pradosha window opens
Perform Abhishek with milk, Panchamrit, or at minimum pure water during Pradosha
Offer Bilva (Bael) leaves — minimum 3, ideally 11 or more
Chant Om Namah Shivaya continuously during the Pradosha window
Apply Vibhuti (sacred ash) to your forehead before the puja and keep it on
On Shani Pradosha: visit a Shani temple first (brief prayer) then Shiva temple for the main puja
donts: Do not eat before the Pradosha puja — observe the fast
Do not cut hair or nails on Trayodashi
Do not offer Tulsi or Ketaki flowers to Shiva — these two flowers are specifically excluded from Shiva worship
Do not arrive late for Pradosha — the 90-minute window is non-negotiable
Do not sleep during the Pradosha window — Shiva's grace flows then; being asleep misses it

hair_nail_non_veg.notes: Trayodashi is Shiva's tithi (connected to Shivaratri on Chaturdashi). All three are forbidden as acts of purification for Shiva's grace.

marathi_calendar_notes.regional_significance: Maharashtra has deep Shiva devotion — the 12 Jyotirlinga temples include Trimbakeshwar (Nashik), Bhimashankar (Pune district), and Aundha Nagnath (Hingoli). Pradosha at any of these Jyotirlingas is extraordinarily powerful.
marathi_calendar_notes.shani_shingnapur_connection: Shani Pradosha draws particular attention in Maharashtra due to the famous Shani Shingnapur temple — on Shani Pradosha, both Shiva and Shani blessings are sought.
marathi_calendar_notes.shravan_pradosha: Pradosha during the Shravan month (holy month of Shiva) carries the combined merit of both Shravan and Pradosha — the most meritorious Pradosha days of the year.

rashifal.note: All signs benefit from Shiva's grace on Pradosha. Saturn-ruled signs (Capricorn, Aquarius) benefit especially on Shani Pradosha.
rashifal.general_guidance: Pradosha is a release day — good for letting go of worries, forgiving grudges, and surrendering burdens to Shiva. Not the best day for strategic planning — better for prayer and spiritual work.
